name: Colorado SNAP Benefits
description: Colorado's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card through county departments of human services. Benefits help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries at authorized retailers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households

name: Health First Colorado (Colorado Medicaid)
description: Colorado's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage to eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, behavioral health, dental, and vision services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 260% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 195% FPL

name: Colorado Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are calculated based on previous wages and can last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on previous wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Colorado Housing and Finance Authority (CHFA)
description: Provides affordable housing programs including homebuyer assistance, down payment assistance, rental assistance, and affordable housing development financing for Coloradans.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Colorado
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Colorado Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Coloradans with offices across the state. Handles cases involving housing, family law, public benefits, immigration, consumer protection, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Colorado residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Colorado Workforce Centers
description: Statewide network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume writing, skills assessments, and training opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: All Colorado job seekers and employers

name: Colorado LEAP - Energy Assistance
description: The Low-Income Energy Assistance Program (LEAP) helps eligible low-income households pay winter heating costs. Benefits are paid directly to the primary heating vendor on behalf of the household.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 60% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Colorado 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Coloradans to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone, text, and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Colorado seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Food Bank of the Rockies
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving the Denver metro area and northern Colorado. Distributes food through a network of over 800 partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and meal programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in northern Colorado and the Denver metro area

name: Care and Share Food Bank for Southern Colorado
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 31 counties in southern Colorado. Distributes food through over 300 partner agencies and programs including mobile pantries.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southern Colorado

name: Colorado Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
description: Serves Colorado veterans and their families with benefits counseling, claims assistance, employment support, veteran trust fund grants, and connections to federal VA programs.
eligibilitySummary: Colorado veterans and their dependents

name: Colorado Crisis Services
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support for anyone experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis. Offers phone, text, and walk-in crisis center services. Free and confidential.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Colorado experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Colorado Child Care Assistance Program (CCCAP)
description: Provides child care subsidies to eligible low-income families to help pay for child care while parents work, seek employment, or attend school. Administered through county departments.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working, seeking work, or in school
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% FPL; varies by county and may be higher

name: Colorado Works (TANF)
description: Colorado's TANF program providing temporary cash assistance and support services to low-income families with children. Emphasizes employment, education, and self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits set by county; generally very low income

name: Colorado WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Child Health Plan Plus (CHP+)
description: Colorado's Children's Health Insurance Program providing low-cost health coverage for uninsured children and pregnant women who earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 and pregnant women with income between 138-260% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Children with family income up to 260% FPL; pregnant women up to 260% FPL

name: RTD Denver - Regional Transportation District
description: Operates bus, light rail, and commuter rail in the Denver metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs through the LiVE discount program for low-income riders.
eligibilitySummary: Denver metro residents; LiVE discount for riders at or below 185% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: LiVE program for households at or below 185% FPL; reduced fares for seniors and disabled
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM

name: Denver Human Services
description: Provides comprehensive social services to Denver residents including food assistance, cash assistance, Medicaid enrollment, child welfare, and adult protective services.
eligibilitySummary: Denver residents in need of social services and benefits enrollment

name: Colorado Springs Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) to low-income families in the Colorado Springs area. Manages public housing communities and rental assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Colorado Springs
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of area median income for Section 8

name: Colorado Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades to reduce energy costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Colorado Division of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Colorado Opportunity Fund (COF)
description: Provides a state-funded stipend to eligible undergraduate students at Colorado public colleges and universities. Reduces the cost of tuition for Colorado residents.
eligibilitySummary: Eligible Colorado resident undergraduate students at public institutions
incomeLimitNotes: No income requirement; available to all eligible Colorado resident undergrads

name: Colorado Adult Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, and workforce readiness at locations across Colorado.
eligibilitySummary: Adults without a high school diploma or needing English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Colorado
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Violence Free Colorado
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence. Connects callers with local shelters, legal advocacy, and support services across Colorado.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Colorado PEAK Benefits Portal
description: Online portal for Coloradans to screen for, apply for, and manage public assistance benefits including SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, LEAP, and child care assistance in one place.
eligibilitySummary: Any Colorado resident wanting to screen for or apply for public assistance benefits
hours: Online portal available 24/7; phone support Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Colorado Transit Assistance Programs
description: Provides transportation assistance for seniors, people with disabilities, and rural residents through regional transit providers and community transportation programs statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors, people with disabilities, and those without access to transportation

name: Disability Law Colorado
description: Provides free legal advocacy for people with disabilities in Colorado. Handles issues including discrimination, access to services, abuse and neglect, education, employment, and housing.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Colorado

name: Boulder County Housing and Human Services
description: Provides social services to Boulder County residents including food assistance, financial assistance, housing support, child welfare, and older adult services.
eligibilitySummary: Boulder County residents in need of social services

name: Colorado Refugee Services Program
description: Provides support services for refugees resettled in Colorado including employment assistance, English language classes, cultural orientation, and case management during the initial resettlement period.
eligibilitySummary: Refugees and certain immigrants in Colorado during initial resettlement period
languages: English, Spanish, and multiple other languages
